coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: You're in week 24 of taper—call this a sharpness day, not a hard day. Run 8–10 miles with 4 × 3min at 10K pace (around 142–145 bpm, based on your recent 136–139 zone) with 2min easy recovery between reps. Stay well short of yesterday's 126 bpm average; this is neuromuscular priming, not lactate work. Your recovery score is 81—solid—so you can afford this, but don't bleed into the long run energy needed for Saturday. Finish with 2 miles easy.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You slept 8 hours with 97% efficiency last night—exceptional. Eat a full breakfast (carbs + protein) 2–3 hours before the run: 75–100g carbs, 20–25g protein. During the run, skip fuel if you're under 90 minutes; post-run, take in 40–50g carbs + 15–20g protein within 30min to drive glycogen repletion before Saturday's long effort. With frequent travel, pack your own bars and electrolyte mix; don't rely on hotel/airport timing.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Your 8+ hours of sleep and 61 bpm resting HR show you're tracking well into taper. Maintain that sleep priority—aim for 8–8.5 hours again tonight, especially with 5 more hard sessions before Slovenia. No data on HRV, so use perceived readiness and that recovery score as your guides. Light stretching or 10min easy walking on your travel day (if you're flying); no sauna or aggressive mobility work until after the race.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 73 days out and in controlled taper—this is when doubt creeps in. Your volume dropped from 57 miles last week to 7.3 so far this week, which is correct, but it *feels* like detraining. It's not. Today's 8–10 miles with tempo reps keeps your aerobic engine primed and your confidence intact without the cumulative fatigue that hammered you in peak. Trust the plan; Saturday's long run will reinforce that you're ready for 2,500m vert in the Alps.
